name: Sweet Almond Pudding with Red Dates
recipeInstructions:
This unusual dish can be served as a dessert, a mid-way course in a large dinner, or as a snack. Pudding may seem misleading as it is actually a thick, hot soup.
1. In a small bowl, mix the rice flour and almond powder. Gradually add cold water to form a thick, smooth paste.
2. Thin the mixture with half a cup of milk. Pour into a heavy saucepan.
3. Add the remaining milk and gently heat until just below boiling point, stirring constantly.
4. Keep stirring to prevent lumping and burning. Turn off the heat and cover. Allow it to cool in place.
5. The first stage can be prepared ahead of time, covered, and refrigerated.
6. Just before serving, reheat the pudding, stirring constantly.
7. When hot, gradually add sugar while continuing to stir.
8. Add sliced dates and cook just under boiling point for one minute.
9. If the pudding becomes too thick (should have the consistency of melted ice cream), add extra milk as needed.
10. Serve in individual bowls hot.
